raw_consumer_statement:
Subject : Dispute of {$21000.00} in Unauthorized Charges & Violation of FCBA/Regulation XXXX I am a victim of identity theft. On or about XX/XX/XXXX, my wallet and mobile device were stolen. I promptly filed a police report with the XXXX, CA Police Department ( # XXXX ) and an FTC Identity Theft Report ( # XXXX ), both of which have been provided to American Express. 

Between XX/XX/XXXX, and XX/XX/XXXX, unauthorized transactions totaling {$21000.00} were made at luxury retailers across XXXX of my American Express accounts ( ending in XXXX, XXXX, XXXX, XXXX, and XXXX ). 

The core of my dispute is based on XXXX irrefutable facts that American Express has ignored : - Physical Impossibility : I was traveling internationally during the entire window these fraudulent " in-person '' transactions occurred. I have provided American Express with passport stamps and flight itineraries proving I was not in the United States, making it physically impossible for me to have authorized or participated in these transactions. 

- Procedural Failure : Under the Fair Credit Billing Act ( FCBA ), creditors must provide documentary evidence upon request if they determine a disputed charge is " correct. '' Despite my written requests, American Express ( specifically via the XXXX XXXX team and representative XXXX XXXX ) has refused to provide any IP logs, geolocation data, or merchant signatures linking me to these charges. Instead, they directed me to their " XXXX XXXX XXXX, '' effectively withholding the evidence required for a fair investigation. 

American Express has closed all XXXX of my accounts and is holding me liable for {$21000.00} in criminal activity. They have labeled my claim as " not in good faith '' without providing a shred of evidence to support that characterization, despite my full cooperation and provision of government-verified theft reports. 

I am attaching all the documents : - Police report on stolen wallet and phone, which were used to make fraud transactions- Identity Theft report, stating I did not authorize any of fraudulent transactions - Flight tickets showing I am not physically present in XXXX to make fraud purchases in XXXX - Email from Amex XXXX team and Amex 's CEO denying the fraud claim as well as evidence linking transaction made by me directly.
